element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation skill; basic flight mechanics and altitude management
Thermals and updrafts; more precision-based than powered flight
Vertical movement and hovering; hover-and-fire mechanics
Free-fall precision landing in target zones
High-speed powered flight with precision control

typical_relief_triggers: Safe altitude maintenance in hover states
typical_tension_triggers: Approach to landing zone with altitude constraints
Precision targeting requirements (skydiving targets, jet pack positioning)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Mission-based, cyclical tension; each vehicle type presents discrete challenge blocks with clear success/failure states. Tension builds through approach phases, peaks at landing/targeting, resolves with mission completion.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus stages accessible after license acquisition; exploration of detailed 3D environments encouraged

difficulty_curve.early_game: Light plane introduction establishes control fundamentals; low-pressure learning environment with forgiving landing zones
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of hang gliding and jet pack mechanics increases precision requirements; bonus tasks (skydiving, human cannonball) add difficulty branches
difficulty_curve.late_game: Rocket belt and attack helicopter bonus content; highest precision and timing demands
notable_spikes: Transition from powered flight (plane/jet pack) to unpowered descent (skydiving) requires fundamentally different mental model
Hang gliding precision with thermal navigation is mechanically distinct from other activities
Attack helicopter bonus stages introduce evasion complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Player earns pilot licenses through sequential completion of distinct aviation activities. Each activity type (plane, hang glider, jet pack, skydiving, rocket belt) functions as a self-contained skill module with its own control scheme and spatial challenges.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Light plane basic flight → hang gliding → jet pack → skydiving progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Core tension in most activities centers on landing accuracy within defined zones. Spatial grammar communicates acceptable landing area through visual markers. Approach phase builds tension; landing zone proximity triggers peak tension.

After license acquisition, player gains access to freely explorable 3D environments (notably miniature US representation in 64 version). Optional objectives reward exploration and encourage non-linear spatial mastery.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Post-license bonus stages and exploration zones

notable_level_patterns.description: Each aviation activity (plane, hang glider, jet pack, parachute, rocket belt) presents unique control mechanics. Players must rapidly context-switch between fundamentally different input-to-movement mappings.
